SA31542.Additional Description: We are an open space for anyone who appreciates Anime or any part of its subculture, like its art, history, games, cosplays, and much more! We watch episodes of student-recommended animes and have discussions in addition to a space to play games (video and board) to build community find friends!

SA78441.Additional Description: Our club offers students a unique opportunity to explore and appreciate local bird species while fostering a love for nature. Members will participate in guided birdwatching trips, learn to identify different species, and contribute to conservation efforts. The club also promotes environmental awareness through educational activities and community involvement. Whether you're an experienced birder or just curious, this club welcomes all levels of interest.

SA66231.Additional Description: ALL students are welcome and can join at any time of the year by simply attending a meeting!

SA11133.Club Officials Emails: Please contact a student leader for more details!

SA10701.Additional Description: Club Israel is an inclusive and welcoming club that celebrates Israel’s culture, history, food, and more.

SA13235.Additional Description: Computer Science (CS Club) offers students a collaborative environment to learn new computer science topics, develop applications individually or as a team, and participate in club-wide competitions and projects. Any and all programming backgrounds are welcome, and projects can range from multi-player video games to complex scientific simulations.

SA73120.Additional Description: To celebrate Eastern European cultures, like Russian, Polish, Ukrainian, Czech, but not limited to! All are welcome!

SA92046.Club Officials Emails: Please contact a student leader for more details!

SA64535.Club: Family, Career and Community Leaders of America

SA55370.Additional Description: FASA is a cultural club aimed to spreading and promoting Filipino culture through weekly meetings, participating in World’s Fair and Night Market, and creating a welcoming community for anyone willing to learn about Filipino culture!

SA86889.Additional Description: Advocacy, civil service, equity and inclusivity

SA27192.Additional Description: FCB is a leadership clubs that helps unite the Freshman Class through large and small events such as Homecoming, Winterfest, etc.

SA91819.Additional Description: Friends International is a club focused on celebrating cultural diversity, fostering inclusivity, and making friends. We provide a space for students of different cultures to share their experiences and learn from each other.

SA52227.Additional Description: Learn more about what it means to be an educator, and get to work with elementary kids!

SA54999.Additional Description:
learn about nursing and participate in activities such as:
 first aid, exploring nursing/medical careers, health/wellness projects

SA48679.Additional Description: Green Team is a student run club which looks to promote a “greener” way of life for the Stevenson community. Students meet weekly to plan events that will make Stevenson and the Lincolnshire community more environmentally aware and friendly.

SA23955.Additional Description: A place where people can come to jam out and play guitars or ukuleles. Don’t worry if you have no experience, anyone is welcome and anyone can learn how to play.

SA69865.Additional Description: Educating about and celebrating Hinduism with everyone possible.

SA68171.Additional Description: Play a variety of tabletop games! We do Magic, Pokémon, board games, TTRPGs (such as D&D), paint miniatures, and more!

SA28279.Additional Description: Club members research current world issues and represent various countries at Model United Nations conferences. Students work individually and in teams to accurately represent their assigned countries in crisis situations and in-depth simulations of current and past United Nations topics.

SA76186.Additional Description: Mongolian clubs goal is to share our culture, traditions, and history. We want to create a welcoming space for everyone, Mongolian or not!

SA92690.Club Officials Emails: Please contact a student leader for more details!

SA15343.Club: National Honor Society of Dance Arts

SA98731.Additional Description:
By eligibility only. Requirements: 

1.Cumulative B+ average in all Spanish classes taken at Stevenson.

2.Completion of 3 service experiences during the 2024-2025 school year.

3.Enrollment in Spanish all four years of high school (including senior year), or enrollment in the highest level of Spanish available by the end of sophomore or junior year.

4.Submission of an application, which will be sent along with the invitation to join if student meets the requirements mentioned above.

SA88896.Additional Description: email Ms. Ruda (jruda@d125.org) for more information!

SA80098.Additional Description: Science Olympiad is a 30-student team that focuses on preparing for and competing in many science-related events that are part of the Science Olympiad competition. The team competes locally, out-of-state, and nationally.

SA83557.Additional Description: Science REACH is a club for students who are interested in scientific research and conducting investigations on topics they’re interested in. Over the year, club members work on conducting their experiments and crafting a research paper on their topic. At the end of the year, members have the opportunity to participate in the Illinois Junior Academy of Science Fairs and other science competitions.

SA34653.Additional Description: SAGA provides a safe space for those in the LGBTQIA+ community and their allies! Come plan events and have fun with friends

SA54280.Additional Description:
SHS Student-Athlete Association is Stevenson's student-athlete advisory committee that represents the leadership body of student-athletes on campus. The mission of SHSSAA is to teach student-athletes how to become leaders both on and off their fields of play. Our goal is to create a safe place where athletes can congregate and discover new ways to make an impact in their communities and high school. SHSSAA will be responsible for social events, community service projects, NSC Conference initiatives and more!

SHSSAA welcomes any student-athlete, administrator or coach who has a desire to attend! SHSSAA is led by Sports Information Coordinator - Allison Fink, Athletic Director - Dwayne Bates, Assistant Athletic Director - Kyle Washburn, and Athletic Coordinator - Regan Carmichael and our Student Executive Board. If you have any questions please contact afink@d125.org.

SA20467.Additional Description:
Stevenson Skatriots is a figure skating club that participate in ice skating performances and hockey games through the school year. Volunteering opportunities occur through the local ice rinks. 

Requirements: Freestyle 1 level / Bronze level or above

SA77135.Additional Description: Spanish Club is a fun learning environment where students of any background can learn about different Spanish-speaking cultures. Activities vary weekly, but fun is guaranteed through different crafts, food, and activities!

SA48909.Day: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday

SA27275.Additional Description: A club to learn all about Taiwanese traditions and culture!

SA60438.Additional Description: We meet as Turkish club with the goal to strengthen the relationships within our club and create a bigger Turkish community. We achieve this by holding meetings where students engage in activities while learning about our culture.

SA71645.Additional Description: This club is for anyone who loves animals! We do a variety of projects, service events, guest speakers, and plan to take field trips in the future.
